schema.appDescription: Calculate GST (Goods and Services Tax) on any amount. Add GST to base price or extract GST from GST-inclusive price. All slab rates covered.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the GST Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: What are the GST slab rates in India 2026?
faq.a: GST has 4 main slabs: 5% (essential goods, basic food items, transport), 12% (processed food, mobiles, medicines), 18% (most services, electronics, restaurants), 28% (luxury goods, cars, tobacco). Basic food items like fresh vegetables, unprocessed grains, and milk are exempt (0%).

faq.q: What is the difference between CGST and SGST?
faq.a: GST is a dual tax: CGST (Central GST) collected by the Centre and SGST (State GST) collected by the state. Each is half the total GST rate. For 18% GST, CGST = 9% and SGST = 9%. For interstate transactions, IGST (Integrated GST) applies instead.

faq.q: Do I need to register for GST?
faq.a: GST registration is mandatory for businesses with annual turnover above ₹40 lakh (₹20 lakh for special category states). Service providers must register above ₹20 lakh turnover. E-commerce sellers must register regardless of turnover.
Yes, the GST Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the GST Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: Restaurant bill calculation (18% GST)
examples.text: A restaurant bill of ₹2,000 attracts 18% GST = ₹360 (CGST ₹180 + SGST ₹180). Total payable: ₹2,360. AC restaurants charge 18%; non-AC restaurants up to ₹7,500 turnover threshold charge 5% GST.

examples.heading: Construction service (18% GST)
examples.text: Construction service costing ₹10 lakh attracts 18% GST = ₹1.8 lakh. Total invoice: ₹11.8 lakh. Note: affordable housing (under ₹45 lakh) attracts concessional 1% GST under specified schemes.
